msgs.text:
I treated the prompt and prior log as a proposal record, not as proof. The required syntax is still the tame-format presentation with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma = tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and constants modulo M=85667662080.The prior log leaves one serious unrefuted candidate, while the scalar, plain-ŌÄ2, and fixed-ŌĄ‚ąí2 variants have recorded finite-quotient failures.

The surviving candidate remains variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2‚ąąZ and ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,where ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of ‚ąíő∑ is immaterial in this word, because that exponent is only applied to ŌĄ, whose finite tame images have odd order.The two algebraic formulas that should stay fixed are these.First, for the sign-cover norm coefficient, let V=kerőļ, let t be the deck involution, and work on a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character block ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1. ForNa (u)=(uŌĄa)ŌÄ,the Fox coefficient isDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j.

It satisfies (1‚ąítőĪa)Da=1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ.Since ŌÄ‚Č°1(mod2) on t, and ŌÄ‚Č°0(mode) on an e-th odd tame character,(tőĪa)ŌÄ=t.

Thus the correct usable formula isDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1.This is the only direction compatible with the unit/radical argument: 1‚ąítőĪa is a 2-adic unit, while 1‚ąít is radical. Hence Da is radical, and the coefficient from x‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑, a unit. So the nontrivial odd tame-character blocks still contribute only unit Smith pivots.

Second, in the trivial tame-inertia block, use the convention(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0,s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf).

If f=2a m with m odd, then s‚Č°1(mod2a) and s‚Č°0(modm). For f even, s is odd, so 1+T‚ą£1+T‚ąís. The valid elimination is the polynomial Ps (T) defined by (1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís.This avoids the recurring reciprocal ambiguity. With h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚ąí1),one gets, for a nontrivial f-th root ő∂,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1).Since 2s+1 is odd and 2s+1‚Č°1(modm), gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Thus h has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, and h(1)=1. With these conventions, the previously claimed checks remain coherent:Ue,fab‚ČÉZ2ef+1‚äēC2,and, for the sign kernel,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÉZ22ef+1‚äēC4.My new point is that the prior instruction ‚Äúcompute the candidate‚Äôs coordinates in Ae,f=H1(P,L2H)‚ÄĚ is not quite well-posed as an absolute statement. That H1 is a space of differences between marked lifts, not a coordinate space for a single lift before choosing an origin.Here is the precise reason. Let f=2a m, let H=‚ü®ŌĄňČ,ŌÉňČ2a‚ü© be the odd Hall subgroup, and P=őĒe,f/H‚ČÉC2a. Let őĪ be a lift of the P-generator acting on the pro-2 kernel, with marked elementőĪ2a=Inn(S),S=ŌÉf.

For a degree-one class v, write the first IA term asmv=őĪ(v)v‚ąí1‚ąąL2.Then the marked power condition gives a nonhomogeneous norm equation:i=0‚ąĎ2a‚ąí1ŌÉňČimv=[S,v].

In particular, for the distinguished trivial class b,NP(mb)=[S,b],not 0 in general. Therefore the raw value őĪ(b)b‚ąí1 of one chosen lift need not lie in kerNP, and so it is not itself an element of H1(P,L2H)=kerNP/im(ŌÉňČ‚ąí1).

If őĪ‚Ä≤ is another lift with the same Frattini action and the same marked S, then the difference mv‚Ä≤‚ąímv satisfies the homogeneous equationNP(mv‚Ä≤‚ąímv)=0.

That difference is what defines a class in H1(P,L2H). So the first IA ambiguity space remains valid, but it classifies the torsor of possible marked lifts after choosing a reference lift. It does not provide absolute coordinates for the candidate alone.This also sharpens the (e,f)=(5,4) target. The seven-dimensional first ambiguity from the prior turns‚ÄĒtwo square directions plus five involution-indexed directions‚ÄĒis a tangent/difference space. To compare the candidate with arithmetic, one must either choose the candidate as origin and compute the arithmetic-minus-candidate class, or choose an arithmetic reference lift and compute the candidate-minus-reference class. Comparing a raw ‚Äúcandidate coordinate vector‚ÄĚ to zero would be a category error unless the origin has already been fixed.For f even, the prior involution-indexed description still looks right as a description of the homogeneous difference space. The nonfree part is(L2H)nonfree‚ČÉJ1‚äē2‚äēJ2 a‚ąí1‚äēI(e,f),I(e,f)=gcd(e,2f/2+1).

The two J1 -summands are the universal square directions b[2]and S[2]. The other summands are indexed by involutionsőīj=ŌĄňČjŌÉňČf/2,j(1+2f/2)‚Č°0(mode),with commutator directionsZj=h‚ąąH‚ąĎ[Xh,Xhőīj].

But these Zj -coordinates are homogeneous difference coordinates. A single marked lift satisfies the affine norm equation above.There is also still the Frobenius-lift gauge. ReplacingŌÉňČbyŌĄňČkŌÉňČ sends j‚Ü¶j+k(2f/2‚ąí1)(mode),so the individual Zj labels are only meaningful after the same tame Frobenius lift is fixed on both the candidate and arithmetic sides.Thus the dynamic candidate remains unrefuted, but the next comparison should be formulated as follows: choose a concrete tame degree-20 field for (e,f)=(5,4), choose a Frobenius lift, choose an origin lift, and compute the difference class in the seven-dimensional homogeneous space. The arithmetic comparison is that this difference class is zero, not that either raw lift has zero coordinates.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains the only unrefuted candidate. The stable formulas areDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1,(1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís.With these conventions, the prior abelianization and sign-kernel conclusions remain consistent. New this turn: H1(P,L2H) classifies differences of marked lifts, not the absolute first IA value of a single lift. A single lift satisfies the affine norm equation NP(mv)=[S,v].

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing ingredient is a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem, or an explicit arithmetic comparison, showing that the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered tame action agrees with the actual arithmetic Frobenius/tame-inertia action over every finite tame subextension.

NEXT: For (e,f)=(5,4), fix a concrete tame degree-20 extension, a Frobenius-lift convention, and an origin lift. Compute the arithmetic-minus-candidate difference class in the two square directions and five involution-indexed Zj -directions. The target is that this homogeneous difference class vanishes.[BREAKTHROUGH]
